counsellor.definition: A person whose job is to listen to people's problems and help them feel better.
counsellor.example: She went to see a counsellor after feeling stressed at work.

refugee.definition: A person who has left their country because of war or danger.
refugee.example: The refugee family moved to a safer country.

empathize.definition: To understand and share another person's feelings.
empathize.example: I tried to empathize with her sadness.

permanent.definition: Lasting for a very long time or forever.
permanent.example: They wanted a permanent home, not a temporary one.

piped.definition: Spoke up in a high or clear voice, often unexpectedly.
piped.example: The little boy piped up with a funny answer.

sums.definition: Describes something perfectly in a short way.
sums.example: That one sentence sums up the whole idea.

sanctuary.definition: A safe, peaceful place where you feel protected.
sanctuary.example: Her bedroom was a sanctuary from the busy world.

thrive.definition: To grow strong, healthy, and successful.
thrive.example: Children thrive when they feel loved and safe.

whoever.example: Whoever comes to the party is welcome.

co-parent.definition: A person who shares the job of raising a child with another parent.
co-parent.example: She and her co-parent talk every week about their son.

siblings.example: He has two siblings, an older sister and a younger brother.

grandparents.definition: The parents of your mother or father.
grandparents.example: My grandparents visit us every summer.

awareness.definition: Knowing that something exists or understanding a situation.
awareness.example: She has good awareness of how her words affect others.

behave.definition: To act in a certain way.
behave.example: The children behaved very well at the restaurant.

appreciation.definition: The feeling of being grateful for something or someone.
appreciation.example: He showed his appreciation by writing a thank-you note.

dump.definition: To throw or put something somewhere carelessly.
dump.example: Don't dump your anger on the people you love.

familial.example: Familial relationships shape who we become.

influential.definition: Having a strong effect on someone or something.
influential.example: Teachers are very influential in a child's life.

determining.definition: Deciding or controlling what something will be.
determining.example: Your early years play a determining role in your personality.

personality.definition: The set of qualities that make a person who they are.
personality.example: She has a warm and friendly personality.

mental.definition: Related to the mind and how you think and feel.
mental.example: Mental health is just as important as physical health.

individuals.example: All children are individuals with their own needs.

structure.definition: The way something is organized or put together.
structure.example: The structure of the family has changed over time.

nuclear.definition: A family with two parents and their children living together.
nuclear.example: Not every child grows up in a nuclear family.

arrangements.definition: Plans or ways things are organized.
arrangements.example: They made special arrangements for the holidays.

conventional.definition: Following the usual or traditional way.
conventional.example: They chose a less conventional way to raise their family.

unconventional.definition: Not following the usual or traditional way.
unconventional.example: Their unconventional lifestyle surprised some people.

commune.definition: A group of people who live together and share things.
commune.example: They live in a commune where everyone shares meals.

cognitive.definition: Related to thinking, learning, and understanding.
cognitive.example: Reading helps with a child's cognitive development.

emotional.example: Children need emotional support from their parents.

partnership.definition: A relationship where two people work together or share their lives.
partnership.example: A strong partnership between parents helps children feel safe.

consideration.definition: Careful thought, or thinking about other people's needs.
consideration.example: They took the children's feelings into consideration.

richness.definition: The quality of being full and valuable.
richness.example: There is great richness in a loving family.

battleground.definition: A place where fights happen; used to describe conflict.
battleground.example: Dinner time should not be a battleground.

veer.example: The conversation veered towards a difficult topic.

preoccupied.definition: Thinking about something so much that you cannot think about other things.
preoccupied.example: She was too preoccupied with worry to enjoy the party.

security.definition: The feeling of being safe and protected.
security.example: Children need a sense of security at home.

curious.definition: Wanting to learn or know about things.
curious.example: Children are naturally curious about the world.

impacts.example: Stress impacts how well a child can learn.

negatively.definition: In a bad or harmful way.
negatively.example: Fighting at home negatively affects children.

concentrate.definition: To focus your attention on something.
concentrate.example: It is hard to concentrate when you are worried.

survey.definition: A study where many people are asked the same questions.
survey.example: The survey asked parents about their daily habits.

factors.definition: Things that cause or affect a result.
factors.example: Many factors affect a child's happiness.

distress.example: The child was in distress after the argument.

carers.definition: People who take care of someone, especially a child or sick person.
carers.example: Parents and carers play an important role in a child's life.

functional.definition: Working well and in a healthy way.
functional.example: A functional family talks about their problems.

visible.example: A child's pain is not always visible to adults.

contributed.definition: Helped to cause something to happen.
contributed.example: Many small things contributed to the problem.

justified.definition: Having a good reason for something.
justified.example: She felt justified in her decision to say no.

helpless.definition: Unable to do anything to change a situation.
helpless.example: He felt helpless when he saw his child cry.

daunting.definition: Making you feel afraid or worried about doing something.
daunting.example: Starting a new job can be daunting.

overwhelming.definition: So strong or powerful that it is hard to deal with.
overwhelming.example: The amount of work felt overwhelming.

interact.definition: To talk to or deal with someone.
interact.example: How parents interact with each other matters to children.

improvements.example: Small improvements in communication can help a lot.

ensure.definition: To make certain that something happens.
ensure.example: We must ensure that children feel safe at home.
